GovCon Giants — 15 Smart Questions to Ask Your APEX Counselor (a practical guide for small businesses navigating government contracts, based on the FREE APEX Accelerator / PTAC service)

1. Can You Help Evaluate My Business? Give a clear picture of who you are, what you do, your experience and goals, then ask whether you're ready to pursue prime contracts or should start as a subcontractor.
2. What Do I Need to Be Successful? Ask them to assess your readiness — certifications, experience, capability statements, teaming partners. Uncovers blind spots and creates a growth roadmap.
3. Can You Help with Market Research? Ask which agencies buy what you sell, how much they spend, and who your competitors are — key to finding hidden opportunities.
4. What Area Are You Most Experienced In? Counselors differ (federal vs state/local); tap their strongest insights.
5. Do You Have Any Contacts in My Industry? Ask for introductions to people at agencies or prime contractors in your niche.
6. What Can I Do to Increase My Odds for Success? Ask what successful businesses like yours do differently — pitch, proposal, events.
7. Are There Local Projects with No Bidders? City/municipal bids with zero or very few responses are golden opportunities.
8. What Meetings or Organizations Should I Join? Industry groups, chambers, procurement events where you build credibility and meet decision-makers.
9. Who is the Most Successful Client You Know? Learn from the best; ask for a warm intro.
10. What Common Mistakes Do You See? Red flags and rookie mistakes — missed deadlines, incomplete proposals, failing to follow up with contracting officers.
11. Can You Recommend Any Free or Low-Cost Training? APEX knows free government-funded webinars, workshops, and courses.
12. What Areas is the Government Investing In Right Now? Current spending trends/priorities (cybersecurity, clean energy, infrastructure, healthcare) so you align your offerings with demand.
13. What Should My Capability Statement Include? It's your government résumé; ask for feedback or a template.
14. Can You Walk Me Through SAM.gov or Other Bid Portals? Ask for a walkthrough so you don't miss opportunities or make costly mistakes.
15. Have You Heard of GovCon Giants? If not, introduce them — free and low-cost education, interviews, and resources for small and minority-owned businesses in government contracting.
